qa_pairs.question: How are H100 SXM5 GPUs partitioned into instances under Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology?
qa_pairs.answer: Under MIG, an H100 SXM5 GPU is partitioned into one instance of 60 Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) (nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_3g.40gb), one instance of 32 SMs (nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_2g.20gb), and two instances of 16 SMs each (nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_1g.10gb).

qa_pairs.question: What happens to some Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) on an H100 SXM5 GPU when it is partitioned using Multi-Instance GPU (MIG)?
qa_pairs.answer: Eight Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) on an H100 SXM5 GPU are left unassigned and effectively lost when the GPU is partitioned under MIG.

qa_pairs.question: How should the partitioning of an H100 GPU under Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) be more accurately described than using 'eighths'?
qa_pairs.answer: Instead of speaking of 'eighths', a MIGed H100 GPU should be considered as divided into 'thirty-thirdths'. For example, what NVIDIA refers to as 'one eighth' is actually 4/33 of an H100 GPU, 'two eighths' is 8/33, and 'three eighths' is 15/33, with 2/33 of the GPU not assigned to any instance.
